_comment: AEGIS Postgres service descriptor. Two paths: (a) Railway's managed Postgres plugin (simplest, single-region), or (b) Neon (recommended — branching, autoscale, point-in-time restore). This file documents the Railway-managed path; for Neon, you only need to wire DATABASE_URL into the API and worker service env vars.
_planTier: Railway Postgres Hobby ($5/mo, 1 GB) is fine for pre-launch. Move to Neon Scale ($69/mo) before announcing GA — point-in-time restore is non-negotiable for an audit-log product.
_image: Railway's managed plugin runs Postgres 16. We pin to PG16 because the Prisma migrations assume features (gen_random_uuid in pgcrypto, GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY) that are stable on 16; do not downgrade.

deploy._healthcheck: Postgres self-reports readiness via `pg_isready`. Railway's plugin handles this internally. If you self-host, mount a readiness probe shell script.
deploy._replicasNote: Postgres primary is single-replica. Read replicas are tracked in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md § 8 'Open architectural questions' — Phase 3 only.

volumes.name: aegis-pg-data
volumes._sizeNote: Start at 10 GB. Audit events accumulate; revisit when used % > 70%.

initialization._runOnce: Railway runs files in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ exactly once on first boot. Mount infra/postgres/init.sql there. Re-running requires a volume reset.

tuning._reference: infra/postgres/postgresql.conf.tuning — apply via Railway's `Postgres Configuration` panel or by mounting a postgresql.conf override.
_criticalKnobs: shared_buffers — 25% of container RAM (Railway exposes the limit as MEMORY_LIMIT_MB).
max_connections — 100 is fine pre-launch; bump if pgbouncer is NOT in front. With pooler, leave at 100 and let pgbouncer multiplex.
work_mem — keep modest (4-8 MB); audit-log queries are mostly sequential reads, no hash joins.

envVars._note: Migration owner. Application connects as `aegis_app` (created by init.sql) — separate role for least privilege.

envVars._provision: Railway generates this when the plugin is created. Rotate via Railway dashboard; remember to update DATABASE_URL on the API + worker after rotation.

envVars._note: C collation is faster and avoids ICU drift across PG versions; we don't need locale-aware sorting on identifiers.

_backups.policy: Railway plugin: snapshots are not automatic. Either (a) configure pg_dump to S3 via a scheduled job, or (b) move to Neon which has continuous backup + PITR. Option (b) strongly recommended before paid GA — losing audit chain history is a compliance event.
